body:
## Purpose
When a surface model’s timestep is longer than `dt_cpl`, it previously received instantaneous turbulent fluxes every coupling step, which breaks conservation over the surface’s own timestep. This PR time-averages turbulent fluxes over each "slow" surface’s dt window before pushing them to that surface’s boundary conditions, while the atmosphere continues to see freshly computed fluxes every `dt_cpl`.

For example, in a simulation where we have `dt_cpl = 6mins` and `dt_ocean = dt_seaice = 30mins`, previously the ocean and sea ice models would receive instantaneous fluxes every 30 minutes. Now, the fluxes will be computed and accumulated every 6 minutes, then averaged at the 30 minute intervals and provided to the ocean/sea ice.

CMIP fluxes look very similar to main before this PR, with general structures the same and minor changes to extrema

Closes #1701

## What this PR does not do
#### integrated land fluxes
Since these fluxes are computed implicitly in the land model, with this implementation they would not be recomputed at the coupler timestep when we have `dt_land > dt_cpl`. This would result in the atmosphere using out-of-date fluxes at some timesteps. E.g. if we have `dt_cpl = 6mins` and `dt_land = 12mins`, the atmos/land fluxes would be recomputed every 12 mins, so on odd timesteps (6, 18, 24 mins, etc) the atmos would be using stale fluxes from the previous coupler dt. In comparison, with the ocean/sea ice/bucket accumulation the fluxes are recomputed with the updated atmosphere state every `dt_cpl`. For now we can avoid this issue by setting `dt_land == dt_cpl`, but it would be good to remove this restriction in the future.

#### precipitation accumulation
This is not currently accumulated across stages in the atmosphere, so accumulating it as we do for the turbulent fluxes here would be incorrect anyway. Once the atmosphere does accumulate correctly over its own step, we can easily set up precipitation accumulation in the same way we do it for turbulent fluxes in this PR.

#### radiation accumulation
Radiation is computed by an infrequent callback (controlled by `dt_rad`), but surface models recompute upwelling longwave radiation as a function of their updated T_sfc at each timestep. This results in an inconsistency where the radiation provided by the atmosphere changes slowly, but surface LW_u changes more quickly. This is a bit different from the flux accumulation being addressed in this PR, so I'll leave it as a piece of future work.

## Content
- [x] add FluxAccumulator object to hold accumulated fluxes for each slow explicit surface
- [x] add flux_accumulators to CoupledSimulation 
- [x] add `sim_dt(sim)` and `will_step(sim, t)` helpers
- [x] checkpoint / restart support for `flux_accumulators`
- [x] add extra scratch space/buffers to BucketSimulation to enable flux accumulation even though its fluxes are computed on the land space (not the boundary space)
- [x] update docs, add unit tests
- [x] add integration tests in experiments/test/fluxes_test.jl

Note: CMIP configs withdt_cpl < dt_ocean/dt_seaice(e.g.cmip_oceananigans_climaseaice*.yml) will have behavior changes
title: enable flux accumulation

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
The benchmark table has been getting cropped so it doesn't display correctly. This PR disables cropping and display size limit.

From the PrettyTables.jl docs:
- fit_table_in_display_horizontally::Bool: If true, the table will be cropped to fit the display horizontally. (Default = true)
- fit_table_in_display_vertically::Bool: If true, the table will be cropped to fit the display vertically. (Default = true)

These are both set to false here.


Since this only affects the benchmarks pipeline, I'll merge without running CI after it's approved.
title: don't crop benchmark table [skip ci]
